critic.verdict: The chart is severely undermined by incorrect structural mapping, leaving the actual 15-second drop completely flat while misplaced syncopations clutter the outro.

issues.problem: The musical drop begins here, but the chart fails to increase density or intensity, continuing the exact same slow pace as the quiet intro.
Misaligned section definitions place the 'drop' at the very end during the outro transition, resulting in awkward syncopations when the music is actually winding down.
Endless, mindless repetition of the same lane rotation (BAR -> NOTE -> GAP) that ignores the actual melodic pitch and shape.
A 1.5-beat hold note is placed on a short synth pluck that has no musical sustain, making the hold feel empty and unrewarding.

design_notes:
The intro sets up a steady synthwave rhythm with comfortable 1-beat steps, introducing outer lane claps and centering the kick on the BAR lane. Short 1.5-beat holds are introduced with simple tap accompaniments to build physical familiarity.

The drop ramps up the energy, delivering a long 2-beat central anchor hold with rhythmic tap syncopations on the outer lanes. This rewards players with a highly flowing, satisfying movement pattern during the track's climax.

The outro cools down the intensity, transitioning from the drop into spacious, nicely spaced single taps that naturally resolve with the fading synthesizer chords.

design_sections.name: Intro
design_sections.intent: Establish the fundamental synthwave pulse with clean, alternating 1.0-beat lane patterns and short holds.

design_sections.name: Drop
design_sections.intent: Maximize physical flow with a long central BAR hold while keeping outer taps clear and responsive.

design_sections.name: Outro
design_sections.intent: Gradually wind down the density to allow players to breathe as the rhythm highway resolves.
